title: Using Podman and Docker Compose [official images & user-level]
body:
/kind bug

## Podman can't find official images

### Description

I have tried to  reproduce steps from @baude article [Using Podman and Docker Compose](https://www.redhat.com/sysadmin/podman-docker-compose). 

As you know in the first example, there are `image: gitea/gitea:latest`. But `sudo docker compose up` returns: 

```sh
ERROR: for gitea-postgres_gitea_1  Cannot create container for service gitea: gitea/gitea:latest: image not known
# Also for postgers:
ERROR: for gitea-postgres_db_1  Cannot create container for service db: postgres:alpine: image not known
```

So, I tied to find them with podman:

```sh
❯ docker search gitea/gitea:latest
Error: 1 error occurred:
	* couldn't search registry "gitea": error pinging docker registry gitea: Get "https://gitea/v2/": dial tcp: lookup gitea: no such host

❯ docker search postgres:alpine
INDEX       NAME                                                          DESCRIPTION                                      STARS       OFFICIAL    AUTOMATED
docker.io   docker.io/technekes/postgres-plus                             postgres:alpine docker image modified to inc...  0                       [OK]
```

Strangest thing, that there no official images from docker hub: [gitea/gitea](https://hub.docker.com/r/gitea/gitea) & [postgres](https://hub.docker.com/_/postgres)

## Podman Docker-compose works in Root-only Mode

### Description

Ok. But what if I will change images path to full?

Actually, I have already done it by follow the prompts in [this article](https://fedoramagazine.org/use-docker-compose-with-podman-to-orchestrate-containers-on-fedora/), but changing the path:

```yaml
version: "3.0"
services:
  db:
    image: docker.io/library/mariadb:latest
    environment: 
      - MYSQL_DATABASE=wpdb
      - MYSQL_USER=wpuser
      - MYSQL_PASSWORD=9x8A7@6b
      - MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD=9x8A7@6b
    volumes:
      - wpdb_vol:/var/lib/mysql
 
  wp:
    image: docker.io/library/wordpress:latest
    volumes:
      - wpsite_vol:/var/www
    depends_on: 
      - db
    ports:
      - 8080:80
  
volumes:
  wpsite_vol: {}
  wpdb_vol: {}
```

And it works, but only with `sudo docker up`. Adding user to `docker` group makes any changes, starting `podman.socket` in user-level mode prevents `docker-compose` from working correctly. Everything sort of works, but the server cannot be accessed. Moreover, port `8080` becomes open by `podman`.

**Listing in user-level mode**:


**Output of `podman version`:**

It's physical machine.

comments.body:
The first issue sounds like a potential registries.conf issue - can you provide a `podman info` as root to try and verify?

For the second issue, with rootless Podman - can you be more clear as to what you are doing / what is going wrong? A reproducer would be ideal.

Actually, I have doing all the steps from that two articles.

But can start from beginning:

So, I have almost fresh installation of Fedora 34 (less than month), have switched from Pop!_OS.
07/13. I needed to work in Docker and I accidentally found on the official website that Fedora Workstation works with OCI out of the box.

After a quick study, I realized that it was podman, did some images on the tutorial, and proceeded to investigate docker-compose.
Here are the commands that I entered:

```sh
# Docker-compose for Podman Setup
sudo dnf install -y podman-docker docker-compose

# pasting yaml from [wp-compose-sample](https://github.com/djnotes/wp-compose-sample/blob/main/docker-compose.yaml)
vim docker-compose.yaml

# enabling useer-level docker
systemctl --user enable podman.socket
systemctl --user start podman.socket
systemctl --user status podman.socket

# adding export DOCKER_HOST=unix:///run/user/$UID/podman/podman.sock
vim ~/.profile
source ~/.profile
docker-compose up
```

But nothing works, then I have changed image path to full, it's have started, but I couldn't have rich server over browser.
Checked at `nmap -Pn 192.168.**.**`, the port `8080` was opened…

That's actually all.

After I have reproduse mostly the same, but from Rad Hat article and with sudo.

Find out that it couldn't find image from docker hub, I thought I should change the order in the list:
```yaml
❯ sudo vim /etc/containers/registries.conf
...
unqualified-search-registries = ["docker.io", "registry.fedoraproject.org", "registry.access.redhat.com", "quay.io"]
```
 I put docker.io first, but nothing changed.

`podman info`:

Isn't this the same as in the `podman info --debug` that I cited above?

Docker-compose will want to connect to the root Docker socket - you need to point it to the rootless Podman socket. `export DOCKER_HOST=unix:///run/user/$UID/podman/podman.sock` where $UID is replaced by the UID of the user running the Podman service should convince it to connect to rootless Podman.

> Docker-compose will want to connect to the root Docker socket - you need to point it to the rootless Podman socket. `export DOCKER_HOST=unix:///run/user/$UID/podman/podman.sock` where $UID is replaced by the UID of the user running the Podman service should convince it to connect to rootless Podman.

But I have done this, when setup user-level podman. Look at sh history section. There are comments of my steps.
To clarify. When I've played with podman, I've used this [Dockerfile](https://github.com/adv4000/docker/blob/master/DockerfileUbuntu.txt#L1-L16), and all works.
Installing podman-docker sets up the link for rootfull compose but not for rootless.

> Installing podman-docker sets up the link for rootfull compose but not for rootless.

So, that's mean I'm able to use `docker-compose` only with sudo?
What kind of group I need to add myself to use it without?
With v3.2 you should be able to use docker-compose against the user socket. You have to export `DOCKER_HOST=///run/user/$UID/podman/podman.sock` to make docker-compose use the user socket.
Can you retest with podman v3.2.3? It contains some fixes for rootless networking.
@Luap99 unfortunately I haven't yet that update. Will write back when have it.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Maybe I'll close it for now.
